articles.title: Painter claiming injury at Bill Belichick’s house sues him over alleged unsafe work site
articles.description:
The painter claims he fell at one of Belichick’s Nantucket homes and is seeking nearly $300,000 in damages.

The post Painter claiming injury at Bill Belichick’s house sues him over alleged unsafe work site appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Painter sues Bill Belichick for nearly $300,000 over alleged unsafe work conditions.
Incident occurred at Belichick's Nantucket home, highlighting safety concerns in local construction.
Potential implications for contractors and homeowners regarding liability and safety standards in Boston.

articles.title: Woman shot and killed by police in N.H.
articles.description:
The Northfield, New Hampshire, shooting marked the third use of deadly force by police in New England since Saturday. 

The post Woman shot and killed by police in N.H. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police shot and killed a woman in Northfield, New Hampshire, on April 6.
This incident marks the third police use of deadly force in New England since Saturday.
Boston residents may feel increased concern over police use of force in the region.

articles.title: Do men or women have worse farts? Science has the answer.
articles.description:
Added bonus: Here’s why air travel tends to make people gassy.

summary: Research indicates men produce more gas than women on average.

Boston residents may experience discomfort during flights due to increased gassiness.

Local residents can draw inspiration for their own home renovations from this design.

articles.title: Savannah Guthrie returns to ‘Today’ show anchor desk
articles.description:
“Here we go, ready or not," Guthrie said as the show opened. “Let’s do the news.”

summary: Savannah Guthrie returned to the 'Today' show anchor desk on April 6, 2026.
Guthrie's return marks her first appearance after a brief hiatus from the show.
Boston viewers can expect refreshed news coverage with Guthrie's leadership.

articles.title: A harrowing race against time to find a downed U.S. airman in Iran
articles.description:
For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

The post A harrowing race against time to find a downed U.S. airman in Iran appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: U.S. Air Force Colonel's jet was shot down in Iran, raising concerns for Boston families.
Military efforts intensified to locate the airman, emphasizing moral obligations to service members.
Potential diplomatic tensions could affect U.S.-Iran relations, impacting local military families in Boston.
